reasoning: The finding claims unauthenticated CRLF injection via the X-HS-WP-Plugin-Proxy-URL header at line 227. Full trace disproves this. $target_url = ProxyUtils::get_proxy_base_url() . $proxy_path. The base URL (get_proxy_base_url -> Filters::apply_sites_proxy_cdn_filters) is built from portal_id (get_option, admin-set numeric DB value) and hublet (get_option, admin-set string), neither visitor-controlled. The $proxy_path comes from two branches: (1) predefined paths: returns $request_uri which was processed through esc_url_raw(wp_unslash($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'])) at line 156 — esc_url_raw() percent-encodes \r and \n to %0D/%0A, eliminating raw CRLF; (2) mapping-derived paths: $hs_path comes from cached remote HubSpot API data (not visitor-controlled), and query string is extracted via wp_parse_url from the already esc_url_raw()-sanitized $request_uri. No unauthenticated visitor can inject raw CRLF into any component of $target_url. The finding's reachability claim ('Unauthenticated — any visitor') is incorrect. The only realistic injection path (REQUEST_URI) is sanitized by esc_url_raw() before use. The theoretical wordpress filter override path requires admin/developer-level code execution, which is not a meaningful attack vector.
